attacking_style: Willing to launch extreme-distance attacks (80km solo) on climbs when rivals show weakness. Opportunistic counterattacker who exploits moments when gruppettos are caught rather than waiting for final kilometers.
team_tactics: Sky employs sustained tempo control to retain multiple domestiques deep into finales (3+ at 25km). Crosswind tactics prioritized over mountain attacks for isolating GC rivals. Team sets brutal pace rather than explosive attacks.
terrain_preference: Gravel climbs and unconventional terrain where audacious long-range efforts can break rival morale. Benefits most when team can control pace on extended climbs rather than punchy gradients.
vulnerability: Struggles when isolated from Sky teammates, particularly on technical descents where crashes/mechanicals cause nervousness. Uncertain form in pre-Tour buildup races where teammates can drop him.
key_patterns: Attacks immediately when sensing rival weakness rather than conservative defense (3 observations)
Executes sustained solo efforts 80km+ from finish, massive tactical gambles (2 observations)
Team retains multiple domestiques exceptionally late into mountain stages for control (2 observations)
Actively seeks bonus seconds even when stage win unlikely, margin hunting (2 observations)
Vulnerable when mechanicals/crashes leave him isolated from team support (2 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: May 2018 Giro shows peak willingness for aggressive racing and sustained power, massive contrast from June 2017 Dauphine weakness
